summary: Multi-character fighter decomp/matching PR covering Crazy Hand, Kirby, and Ice Climbers/Popo/Nana code. It replaced many `/// #...` stubs with matching C, corrected fighter/item headers and attribute structs, and added item/effect declarations needed by the new implementations. decomp-dev reported matched code rising to 60.76% (+0.18%, +7136 bytes), with 20 new 100% matches and 8 partial improvements; PR body and human review comments were absent.

key_files.role: Major Crazy Hand matching work: wait/AI selection, player-button dispatch, bomb spawning, grab/capture setup, and typed action-selection table.
Corrected several Crazy Hand dat-attribute offsets from s32 to float for position/velocity/offset arithmetic.
Implemented Kirby hat/special-N routines including `ftKb_UnkIntBoolFunc0` and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1BAC`.
Fixed Kirby special-N/Yoshi helper prototypes, including adding `Fighter_GObj*` to `ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0` and removing unused gobj args from several getter declarations.
Implemented Kirby/Yoshi capture animation handlers and `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`; added effect/item includes and matching pragmas/padding.
Large Ice Climbers/Popo matching pass for side-B string handling, up-B start/throw physics and collisions, and down-B/blizzard accessory behavior.
Filled Ice Climber attribute holes with named float offsets x7C, x9C, xA0, xB8, xBC, and xC0.
Small stack-padding changes improving Nana SpecialS collision matches.
Declared `ftNn_Init_8012300C`, used by Popo/Nana range checks.
Added `itClimbersStringAttributes` layout for Ice Climbers string item command data.
Exposed missing climbers-string item functions `it_802C3864` and `it_802C3950`.

what_changed: Replaced Crazy Hand stubs with C for `ftCh_Init_80156198`, `ftCh_Init_80156310`, `ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim`, `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`, `fn_801588B8`, and `fn_8015B548`; bot report shows all but `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` reached 100%, with `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` at 98.86%.
Typed `ftCh_Init_803D4878` as `struct ftCh_Init_803D4878_t` with `states[18]`, `x48[20]`, `u8_pair x5C[6]`, and `x68[32]`, replacing a flat anonymous `asids/bytes` table.

Crazy Hand wait logic now branches on `Player_GetPlayerSlotType`; slot type 0 uses `HSD_PadMasterStatus[3]` button combinations through `btn_pressed`, while the other path uses boss timers/random tables and dispatches `ftMh_MS_*` actions.
Crazy Hand bomb logic in `fn_801588B8` spawns `It_Kind_CrazyHand_Bomb` from multiple hand/body joints via `it_802F0F6C` and plays SFX when command variables are set.
Crazy Hand capture setup in `fn_8015B548` uses common grab/capture helpers, sets victim references/callbacks, changes motion state, hides the fighter, and updates hit/collision state.

Kirby work implemented `ftKb_UnkIntBoolFunc0`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1BAC`, four Yoshi capture animation handlers, and `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`; it also simplified several Yoshi-special getter functions to take no `gobj` argument.
Popo/Nana code now uses `ftNn_Init_8012300C` and `Player_GetEntityAtIndex(player_id, 1)` to check Nana availability/range before up-B partner behavior.
Item headers gained `itClimbersStringAttributes` and climbers-string prototypes so Popo side-B code can read item special attributes and call item state functions.

decomp-dev reported 20 new matches, including `ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim`, `fn_801588B8`, `fn_80122D2C`, `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`, `ftKb_UnkIntBoolFunc0`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Phys`, and `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Coll`.
smart_moves: The Crazy Hand action table was not left as opaque bytes: splitting it into state IDs, selection ranges, `u8_pair` entries, and random-transition bytes made the AI-selection code expressible while preserving layout.
Attribute struct corrections were tied directly to observed arithmetic: Crazy Hand and Popo offsets used as positions, velocities, distances, or timers were promoted from padding/s32 to float fields.

Header fixes were made alongside implementations, preventing signature drift; notably `ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0` gained the `Fighter_GObj*` argument used at call sites, while pure Kirby Yoshi attribute getters dropped unused parameters.
Matching-sensitive helper code was localized: `btn_pressed`, `doAnim0`, `doAnim1`, `checkNanaInRange`, `ftPp_set_cbs`, and small physics helpers reduce duplication without forcing broad renames.
The Popo custom `my_sqrtf` uses `__frsqrte`, three Newton-style refinements, stack padding, and a volatile float return path, showing a targeted PPC codegen tactic rather than a semantic rewrite.
`#pragma dont_inline` around `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`, `PAD_STACK` calls, dummy `u8` arrays, and retained odd control-flow comments were used only where matching required them.
Cross-system dependencies were added explicitly: Crazy Hand bomb item, Kirby/GameWatch/Yoshi effects/items, Ice Climbers blizzard/string items, and common fighter helpers.
decomp_lessons: When a dat-attribute offset participates in float math or vector construction, prefer correcting the struct field type over casting at use sites; this PR did that for Crazy Hand and Ice Climber attributes.
Opaque data tables often need intermediate structs to match cleanly. The `ftCh_Init_803D4878_t` split is a reusable pattern for tables that mix state IDs, index ranges, and random transition pools.
Partner-character code should guard entity and joint access with `Player_GetEntityAtIndex` null checks and motion-state ranges; the Popo/Nana up-B throw code demonstrates this.
Do not keep unused parameters in prototypes once evidence shows the function reads globals only; Kirby Yoshi special getters became `void`-argument functions.
Conversely, add object parameters when callbacks/state initializers actually need fighter context; `ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0` was corrected to accept `Fighter_GObj*`.
For matching, stack shape matters: `PAD_STACK`, dummy locals, and even `gobj->user_data` versus `GET_FIGHTER` can be deliberate codegen choices, not cleanup opportunities.
Effect and item spawning routines often force header work first: add the item attribute struct/prototypes so fighter logic can use the real item APIs instead of placeholder declarations.
Partial matches and data regressions should be tracked honestly; this PR still left `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` at 98.86%, Popo start/down-B routines below 100%, and two small `.sdata2` regressions.
naming_conventions: `ftCh_` is used for Crazy Hand functions, with remaining unknowns kept as address-suffixed names such as `fn_801588B8` and `fn_8015B548`.
`ftKb_` prefixes Kirby work; Kirby copied-special/Yoshi functions keep long descriptive names like `ftKb_YsSpecialAirNCapture1_Anim` rather than speculative semantic renames.
`ftPp_` covers Popo/Ice Climbers shared routines, while Nana declarations live under `ftNn_`; partner checks still call `ftNn_Init_8012300C` from Popo code.
Motion state names use existing enums such as `ftMh_MS_*`, `ftKb_MS_*`, and `ftPp_MS_*`; numeric states remain where the diff did not rename them.
Attribute structs retain offset-style field names (`x7C`, `x9C`, `xB8`, etc.) until semantics are known, with `_pos` suffixes used for known vector-like fields.
Item APIs retain address-style names where unknown (`it_802C3864`, `it_802C3950`) and use typed attribute names when layout is known (`itClimbersStringAttributes`).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8/12/16/48)` and local dummy arrays such as `u8 _[16]`, `u8 _[4]`, and `u8 __[20]` to tune stack frame layout.
Added `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` around `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim` to prevent unwanted inlining for the matched function.
Implemented `my_sqrtf` with PPC `__frsqrte`, repeated refinement, static double constants, and `volatile float y` to match generated sqrt code in Popo side-B velocity math.
Kept some direct `gobj->user_data` accesses and even a `/// @todo #GET_FIGHTER` note where macro expansion would alter codegen.
Used typed table fields and `u8_pair` indexing for Crazy Hand random action selection instead of pointer arithmetic over an anonymous byte blob.
Used ternary assignment plus `PAD_STACK(16)` in `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90` and `ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C06C`, improving both functions to 99.82%.
Preserved suspicious original control flow in `ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0`, including an `end` label with a comment that original code returns without a value.

review_feedback.note: Reported GALE01 matched code 60.76% (+0.18%, +7136 bytes), 20 new matches, 8 improvements in unmatched items, and 2 `.sdata2` regressions.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice, and the PR body was empty. The squash auto-merge message listed `ftKirby work`, `ftCh_Init.c work`, and `Ice Climbers work`.

follow_up_queries: ftCh_Init_803D4878 u8_pair Crazy Hand action selection
ftKb_SpecialN_800F1BAC Kirby hat kind callbacks SFX
ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0 itClimbersStringAttributes original code returns without value
